Olympus Corporation Announces Executive Changes , Effective April 1, 2026
data.description:
Olympus Corporation announced changes in executive officers, with Simone Berger appointed as Executive Officer and Chief Human Resources Officer, and David Shan as Executive Officer and Chief Manufacturing and Supply Officer, effective April 1, 2026. Shigeto Ohtsuki and Tetsuo Kobayashi will be stepping down from these roles. Shigeto Ohtsuki has made significant contributions to Olympus since joining in 2019, primarily focusing on HR and general affairs while also taking on regional and global management responsibilities.

As part of Transform Olympus, he has strongly driven initiatives that reflect the standards of a global MedTech company. These include the implementation of a globally aligned, job-based HR system and performance management frameworks, as well as the realization of global assignments that place the right people in the right positions across regions and countries. Through these efforts, he has led the establishment of a solid foundation for the Company as a global MedTech company.

With these comprehensive institutional reforms reaching a significant milestone, he has expressed his intention to step down. Simone Berger, who will succeed him, brings extensive global experience from Germany, Singapore, and China. She has led major culture and people transformation initiatives across industries and successfully managed organizational development in matrix environments where HQ and regions work closely together.

Simone drives HR as a business function with a clear focus on value creation and ROI. Her execution focused enterprise leadership will accelerate these efforts, foster innovation, and deliver new value to our business. She will be a strong ambassador to drive growth mindset across our organization and help to accelerate our strategy 'Where Purpose Meets Performance.'

Tetsuo Kobayashi has driven the strengthening and streamlining of global production systems. As part of Transform Olympus, he has also led the reorganization of manufacturing bases and the optimization of the supply chain, contributing to the advancement of quality control and the enhancement of cost competitiveness. Under the new Corporate Strategy starting from April 2026, achieving our growth objectives and transforming the operating model will require integrated management of extensive manufacturing and supply chain network, along with accelerated operational reforms. Having completed these preparations, he has expressed his intention to step down. The successor, David Shan brings over 30 years of experience at leading global companies, spanning not only manufacturing and supply chain but also R&D, engineering, and Quality.

He has successfully driven standardization, automation, and transformation of operations on a global scale. Leveraging his exceptional leadership and a strong academic and professional background cultivated across multiple countries and regions, we expect him to lead the establishment of a streamlined organizational structure and operating model?transitioning from a region-centric approach to a business-centric one. His ability to align diverse teams, optimize processes, and drive efficiencies will be instrumental in strengthening our global operations and enabling sustainable, long-term growth.

Simone Berger was born on June 12, 1978. Her career summary includes: December 2000 Joined Bayer Aktiengesellschaft; October 2005 Manager, International Human Resources Asia/Pacific, Schaeffler Holding (China) Co. Ltd.; January 2010 Senior Manager, Human Resources Asia/Pacific, Schaeffler (Singapore) Pte.

Ltd.; October 2011 Regional HR Manager Asia/Pacific, Voith Turbo Pte. Ltd.; August 2012 Senior Vice President, Global Human Resources, Goodbaby International Holdings Limited; December 2015 Managing Director, Goodbaby (Europe) GmbH & Co. KG; January 2019 Chief People Officer, STADA Arzneimittel AG (Present); April 2021 Member of the STADA Arzneimittel AG Executive Board (Present); April 2026 Join Olympus Europa SE & Co.

KG (plan); Executive Officer and Chief Human Resources Officer, Olympus Corporation (plan). David Shan was born on July 27, 1970. His career summary includes: June 1995 Engineer, Technical Service, Siem Supranite S.A.S.; January 1996 Engineer, R&D and Technical Service Europe, 3M COMPANY; February 2000 Manager, R&D Technical Service Europe and Plant Quality Manager, 3M COMPANY; December 2002 Director, Quality & Safety, European Business Unit, Graham Packaging Company, L.P.; May 2005 Director, Quality, Household, Chemical & Automotive Business Unit, Graham Packaging Company, L.P.; February 2006 Senior Director, Global Quality, Graham Packaging Company, L.P.; September 2007 Vice President, Global Operational Excellence, Graham Packaging Company, L.P.; April 2010 Industrial Director, Manufacturing & Productivity, Europe, Bunge Limited; January 2011 Group Vice President, Operations Perfumery and Operations Excellence, SGD Pharma; September 2013 Chief Operating Officer, SGD Pharma; October 2015 Vice President, Worldwide Pharmaceutical Systems Operations, Becton, Dickinson and Company; October 2017 Senior Vice President, Worldwide Device Operations, Becton, Dickinson and Company; May 2018 Senior Vice President, Global Supply Chain, Becton, Dickinson and Company; March 2020 Executive Vice President & Chief Quality Officer, Becton, Dickinson and Company; February 2023 Executive Vice President & Chief Integrated Supply Chain Officer, Becton, Dickinson and Company (present); April 2026 Join Olympus Schweiz AG (plan); Executive Officer, Chief Manufacturing and Supply Officer, Olympus Corporation (plan).

data.title: TVS Motor Company Limited Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended December 31, 2025
data.description:
TVS Motor Company Limited reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended December 31, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported sales was INR 147,555.2 million compared to INR 110,348.8 million a year ago. Revenue was INR 147,452.2 million compared to INR 110,999.9 million a year ago. Net income was INR 8,412.9 million compared to INR 5,660.3 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 17.71 compared to INR 11.82 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 17.71 compared to INR 11.82 a year ago. Basic earnings per share was INR 17.71 compared to INR 11.91 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share was INR 17.71 compared to INR 11.91 a year ago.
For the nine months, sales was INR 410,167.9 million compared to INR 325,465.4 million a year ago. Revenue was INR 410,321.5 million compared to INR 326,847.3 million a year ago. Net income was INR 22,468.1 million compared to INR 15,874 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 47.29 compared to INR 33.01 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 47.29 compared to INR 33.01 a year ago. Basic earnings per share was INR 47.29 compared to INR 33.41 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share was INR 47.29 compared to INR 33.41 a year ago.

Indonesian Stocks Tumble as MSCI Freeze Raises Red Flags
data.description:
By Amanda Lee and Ying Xian Wong


Indonesian stocks slid Wednesday after global index provider MSCI Inc. flagged investability issues that could lead to a potential downgrade of the Southeast Asian market's status.

The Jakarta Composite Index fell as much as 9% during the day, before paring losses to 7.7%. It is on track for its biggest one-day loss since April 2025.

MSCI will temporarily freeze certain index-related changes for Indonesian securities over what it said were significant investor concerns about transparency in company ownership.

The move could spark a flight of capital out of Indonesia, where government policy has already generated worries about fiscal risks, shaking investor confidence in the market.

Another headwind came from President Prabowo Subianto's nomination of his nephew as a deputy governor at Bank Indonesia, which has raised questions about the central bank's independence.

Speaking at the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, last week, Subianto said that his administration's social initiatives--including the flagship "free lunch" program--aren't populist measures, but productivity-improving ones that will "save billions of dollars" in the long term.

Indonesia is determined to become a global economy and will always be open to foreign investment, he added.

Under the MSCI freeze, Indonesian market authorities have until May to provide more granular, reliable information on shareholding structures to address concerns around transparency and "possible coordinated trading behavior that undermines proper price formation," the index provider said.

If improvements aren't made, MSCI will reassess Indonesia's market accessibility status. That could lead to Indonesia being reclassified as a "frontier market," a rung below its current emerging-market status.

Indonesian securities' weighting on MSCI Emerging Markets Indexes could also be reduced.

Indonesian regulators said they will work with MSCI to address its feedback and improve the credibility and global standing of the country's capital market. Recognizing MSCI index weightings as an important reference for investors, they said efforts underway include monthly disclosure of free-float data.

In a report last week, Goldman Sachs strategists estimated that a 26%-27% lowering of the free?float market cap of MSCI Indonesia would likely lead to about $2.3 billion of outflows.

As these outflows are redistributed across MSCI markets, other emerging economy peers would likely receive most of the inflows--particularly within Asia, where China, Taiwan, India and South Korea would likely benefit, GS said.

Until the clouds clear, Indonesia equities may experience more volatility, but Jeffrosenberg Chen Lim at Maybank Sekuritas remains confident in the market's long-term prospects.

The rally in precious and industrial metals is a boon for the commodity-rich economy, and its growth outlook remains solid, the head of research said.

Given that capital-market regulators are making efforts to improve disclosure, he thinks they will meet MSCI's requirements, avoiding a downgrade.

Maybank maintains its end-2026 target for the JCI at 9250, compared with 8646.94 end-2025.

MSCI said it "will continue to monitor developments in the Indonesian market and engage with market participants and authorities."


Write to Amanda Lee at amanda.lee@wsj.com and Ying Xian Wong at yingxian.wong@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

01-28-26 0359ET

data.title: Turkey urges US to resolve Iran disputes one by one, says Iran ready for nuclear talks
data.description:
ANKARA, Jan 28 (Reuters) - Turkey's foreign minister on Wednesday urged the U.S. to resolve its disputes with Iran "one by one" instead of through a sweeping deal, saying this would avoid humiliating Iranian officials, and added that Tehran was ready for talks on its nuclear programme.

U.S. President Donald Trump said last week that Washington had an "armada" heading toward Iran but hoped he would not have to use it, as he renewed warnings to Tehran against killing protesters or restarting its nuclear programme.

Tehran brutally cracked down on anti-government protests earlier this month, leading to the death and arrest of thousands of people. Officials blamed the unrest on "armed terrorists and rioters" linked to Iran's foes, the United States and Israel. Rights groups describe the protests as the biggest since the 1979 revolution.

Speaking to Al Jazeera, Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan reiterated that Turkey opposed any foreign intervention or attack on Iran, saying it would be "wrong to start the war again."

"My advice always to the American friends: close the files one by one with Iranians. Start with nuclear, close it, then the other, then the other," Fidan said.

"If you put them as a package all of them, it will be very difficult for our Iranian friends to digest," he said. "It sometimes might seem humiliating for them. It will be very difficult to explain to not only themselves, but also to the leadership."

In June, the United States struck Iran's nuclear facilities amid heightened regional tensions with Israel over the war in Gaza. Talks on Tehran's nuclear programme - which it says is for peaceful purposes - have made little progress.

Turkey, a NATO member that shares a border with Iran, has said it reached out to both U.S. and Iranian officials. Ankara has said Tehran must be allowed to handle its domestic issues on its own, warning that any destabilisation would exceed the region's capacity to manage at this time.

Fidan has also said Israel was still looking to attack Iran.

(Reporting by Tuvan Gumrukcu; Editing by Daren Butler and Ros Russell)

First-class goods in second-tier cities as luxury goes local in China
data.description:
SHANGHAI, Jan 28 (Reuters) - China's so-called second-tier cities are fast becoming the first stop for luxury goods vendors as middle-class consumers seek high living standards in lower-cost locales, taking with them their penchant for pricey parkers and expensive extras.

With luxury spending in places like Nanjing, Changsha and two dozen other middling cities exceeding that of a handful of economic powerhouses such as Beijing and Shanghai, bling brands like Burberry and Louis Vuitton owner LVMH are following the money and booking sales that point to a recovery in China's battered luxury sector.

"The fact that you have all these second-tier cities now in the top 10 (luxury sales) ranking - it's crazy if you think about it," said Zino Helmlinger, head of China retail at CBRE.

China accounts for roughly a quarter of luxury spending but sales have been sluggish since the end of a post-pandemic boom while weak economic growth and fallout from a property sector crisis continue to trickle down to the shopper on the street.

However, Burberry last week said China's Generation Z helped revenue beat analyst expectations while LVMH on Tuesday flagged a recovery in China with forecast-beating fourth-quarter sales.

Notably, in August, when Louis Vuitton launched beauty line "La Beauté Louis Vuitton" in China, it made its eye shadow, lip balm and 1,200 yuan ($172) lipstick first available not in a first-tier city but at Nanjing Deji Plaza.

Months earlier, data showed Nanjing Deji Plaza had, for the first time, leapfrogged long-time luxury mall leader Beijing SKP to become China's top-performing high-end shopping centre.

The mall, in the Jiangsu provincial capital of 9.5 million people, booked sales of more than 24.5 billion yuan in 2024 compared to Beijing SKP's 22.2 billion yuan, state media said. Moreover, it likely stayed at the top in 2025, analysts said.

The mall has an art museum, modern food hall and 500 square metre (5,382 square feet) restrooms with themes such as calligraphy, classical music and cyberpunk.

   So elaborate are the restrooms that they have gone viral on social media, and brands including Self-Portrait and Estee Lauder's MAC Cosmetics have had pop-up shops in them.

"There are many delicious types of food and the selection of shops is excellent," 24-year-old Zhou Shiyong said of Nanjing Deji Plaza. "Only Deji has this kind of assortment; other shopping malls don't have it, which is why we come to Deji."

DEJI 'DOMINATES COMMERCIAL EFFICIENCY'

Second-tier cities such as Nanjing are becoming increasingly important to luxury brands as a growing contingent of middle-class people shun more economically developed first-tier cities such as Beijing and Shanghai to benefit from lower living costs.

Latest research from insights firm MDRi showed luxury shoppers in second-tier cities spent an average of 253,800 yuan in 2024, up 22% from the previous year and surpassing first-tier consumers, whose spending fell 4% to 250,200 yuan.

    Top brands are chasing these consumers as they move further afield from previous growth markets and, in the case of Burberry, trying out new methods of marketing such as setting up a branded ice rink and a pop-up shop on a ski slope.

"Recent earnings suggest a modest recovery, and part of that is due to more active investment - flagship experiences in first-tier cities, and more targeted, performance-led strategies in the top malls in lower-tier cities," said James Macdonald, head of Savills research for China.

Deji, owned by real estate conglomerate Deji Group, is the Nanjing region's only mall to house every major luxury brand. It also offers more accessible labels aimed at Gen Z shoppers - an increasingly powerful force in the luxury sector as brands seek to tap shifting tastes among fickle younger consumers.

"Deji has the highest luxury sales density in China. They have an ultra-strong VIP ecosystem, deep brand partnerships, frequent store upgrades and they basically dominate commercial efficiency," said CBRE's Helmlinger.

"Brands would rather wait for a location there than go to another project just a few kilometres away."

MALLS IN SECOND-TIER CITIES CLIMB LUXURY RANKS

Malls in other second-tier cities - such as Changsha IFS, Wuhan Wushang and Hangzhou In77 - are also rising in luxury sales ranking, Helmlinger said.

Their ascendancy is partly economic. McKinsey research released last year showed that, in China, consumers in the biggest cities were most likely to cut discretionary spending.

Consumer confidence was stronger among young and middle-income shoppers in second-tier cities, where living costs are lower and local job security firmer, the research showed.

Many second-tier cities have also seen their middle-class population boosted by a net inflow of people from top-tier centres, Savills' Macdonald said.

Demographic and economic shifts aside, Helmlinger said top malls in second-tier cities have significantly improved their offerings, giving nearby consumers access to brands without having to travel to Shanghai or Beijing.

"It really shows China is going through a wide change in consumer behaviour, and in where money is localised and spent," said Helmlinger. "In the coming few years we're going to see many more second-tier cities rising, because that's where the money is."

($1 = 6.9554 Chinese yuan renminbi)

(Reporting by Casey Hall; Additional reporting by Xihao Jiang and Chenxi Yang in Shanghai; Editing by Anne Marie Roantree and Christopher Cushing)

By Casey Hall

U.S. Futures Slightly Up, European Stocks Mixed
data.description:
U.S. futures for the S&P 500 were up 0.3% and futures for the Dow Jones Industrial Average were flat. Changes in futures do not necessarily predict movements after the opening bell.

In Europe, the Stoxx Europe 600 was flat in morning trading. ASML Holding climbed 6% and Infineon Technologies rose 5.2%. On the other hand, LVMH Moet Hennessy sunk 7.4%, and Christian Dior dropped 7%. The FTSE 100 added 0.1%. Other stocks in Europe were mixed as France's CAC 40 decreased 0.8% and Germany's DAX was flat.

The Wall Street Journal Dollar Index rose 0.2% to 93.81.

In commodities, Brent crude was flat at $66.58 a barrel, and WTI crude gained 0.2% to $62.49 a barrel. The European benchmark price for natural gas, the Dutch futures contract TTF, was up 0.4% to 38.92 euros a megawatt hour.

The German 10-year Bund yield fell by 2 basis points to 2.862% from 2.877%, and the yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury declined by 1 basis point to 4.239% from 4.25%. Bond prices and yields move in opposite directions.

Stocks in Asia were mixed as Japan's Nikkei 225 index was flat, whereas Hong Kong's Hang Seng climbed 2.6%. China's benchmark Shanghai Composite gained 0.3%.


-This article was automatically generated by MarketWatch.


Write to Barcelona Editors at barcelonaeditors@dowjones.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

01-28-26 0350ET

data.title: Airbus Wins Order for 31 Aircraft From Delta Air Lines
India's Maruti Suzuki misses quarterly profit view on slower exports growth, one-time charge
data.description:
Jan 28 (Reuters) - Maruti Suzuki, India's top carmaker by sales, posted a smaller-than-expected quarterly profit on Wednesday, hurt by lower export proceeds, which partly offset a jump in domestic sales.

The Swift small car manufacturer reported a profit of 37.94 billion rupees ($413.5 million) for the October-December period, up from 35.25 billion rupees a year before, but fell short of analysts' expectation of 42.61 billion rupees, per data compiled by LSEG.

The quarterly results also included a one-time charge of 5.94 billion rupees tied to India's newly-enacted labour codes.

Maruti kicks off the earnings season for Indian automakers in a quarter where car sales hit a record high thanks to the country's tax cuts that made most models more affordable.

($1 = 91.7437 Indian rupees)

(Reporting by Nandan Mandayam in Bengaluru; Editing by Mrigank Dhaniwala)

Post-war recovery agreement with US needs more work, Zelenskiy says
data.description:
Jan 28 (Reuters) - Ukraine has identified areas in an agreement with the United States on post-war recovery that need to be worked through in more depth, President Volodymyr Zelenskiy said on Wednesday.

The recovery agreement is part of wider deal on ending the nearly four-year war with Russia.

"Work with the American side is progressing actively, and on the Ukrainian side we are working with maximum efficiency," Zelenskiy said on X.

(Reporting by Anna Pruchnicka; Editing by Sharon Singleton)

Iran says it didn't request negotiations with U.S., foreign minister says
data.description:
DUBAI, Jan 28 (Reuters) - Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi denied being in contact with U.S. special envoy Steve Witkoff in recent days or requesting negotiations, state media reported on Wednesday.

The foreign minister added Tehran remains in contact with mediating countries that are "holding consultations".

(Reporting by Dubai Newsroom; Editing by Sharon Singleton)
